title: Ηθικά Νικομάχεια, Τόμος Δεύτερος
summaries: "Ηθικά Νικομάχεια, Τόμος Δεύτερος" by Aristotle is a philosophical work from ancient Greece. This treatise explores the science of the good for human life—examining how people should best live and what actions should aim toward. Aristotle approaches ethics as practical rather than merely theoretical, seeking to help readers achieve goodness. The work profoundly influenced medieval European philosophy, law, and theology, and later inspired the modern virtue ethics movement. (This is an automatically generated summary.)
